name: Potential OGNL Injection Exploitation In JVM Based Application
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Moti Harmats
SIGMA Title: Potential OGNL Injection Exploitation In JVM Based Application
SIGMA Description: Detects potential OGNL Injection exploitation, which may lead to RCE.
OGNL is an expression language that is supported in many JVM based systems.
OGNL Injection is the reason for some high profile RCE's such as Apache Struts (CVE-2017-5638) and Confluence (CVE-2022-26134)


False positives:
- Application bugs

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%org.apache.commons.ognl.ognlexception%' OR LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%expressionsyntaxexception%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Potential OGNL Injection Exploitation In JVM Based Application on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event matches LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%org.apache.commons.ognl.ognlexception%' OR LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%expressionsyntaxexception%' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Potential OGNL Injection Exploitation In JVM Based Application
